title: Agent Trace Entry
description: Discriminated union of trace events emitted during pi-jit-agents executeAgent invocations for monitor-classify pipelines. Each entry validates a single JSONL line; the .jsonl file as a whole is not a single JSON document. Per DEC-0004 the structural shape ({ type, id, parentId, timestamp, ...extra }) mirrors pi-coding-agent's SessionEntry but is deliberately a separate schema namespace — there is no literal inheritance. Per DEC-0005 these entries are produced by a push-write trace stream divergent from pi-mono's pull/replay model, so each variant is fired at the moment of occurrence inside executeAgent rather than reconstructed after the fact. The six variants form a parent-chained tree: session_start (root) → classify_call → { classify_response, context_collection } → verdict_decision; trace_end closes the session at the executeAgent exit boundary.

traceId.description: ULID — 26-character Crockford base32, lexicographically sortable, monotonic-by-timestamp. Chosen over UUIDv4 because trace lines are append-ordered and parent/child pairs benefit from sortable IDs; chosen over a monotonic counter because ULIDs do not require shared-state coordination across concurrent executeAgent calls.

isoTimestamp.description: ISO 8601 timestamp captured at event emission.

verdict.description: Final classification verdict. clean/flag/new mirror the verdict.schema.json contract; error indicates the dispatch itself failed before a verdict could be produced.

contentBlock.description: Permissive content-block shape. pi-ai's content payload schema evolves (text, thinking blocks, tool calls with arbitrary arguments); we pin the discriminator field but leave the rest open so trace capture does not break on upstream additions.

parentId.description: session_start is the trace root; parentId is always null.

sessionId.description: Identifier for the trace session — typically the same ULID as id, used as a foreign key from downstream variants for filesystem-level grouping.

monitorName.description: Monitor name when the agent is dispatched as a monitor classifier; null or absent for non-monitor traces.

renderedPrompt.description: Post-Nunjucks-render prompt as actually sent to the model.

inputText.description: Primary input under classification — typically the assistant_text collector payload.

parentId.description: References the parent classify_call.id; one context_collection entry is fired per resolved collector.

collectorId.description: Collector identifier, e.g., assistant_text, recent_files, conversation_window.

collectedValue.description: Permissive — collectors return strings, arrays, or objects depending on collector kind. No constraint imposed at the trace layer; redaction occurs at TraceRedactor (T2) before this field is written.

mappingDecisionRationale.description: Brief description of the verdict-mapping path — which tool-call argument or fallback rule produced the verdict.

parentId.description: References the parent session_start.id; trace_end closes the session at executeAgent exit.
